evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Data quality, validation, monitoring, and observability skills
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals dominate throughout requirements — data quality, validation, monitoring, observability, CI/CD, query optimization — with no velocity phrases present; classified as rigour.
evidence.domain_risk: Portuguese consulting company specializing in IT services; end client unnamed
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Consulting/IT services firm with unnamed end client and no financial or regulatory language in JD; defaulting to moderate given generic SaaS/enterprise context.
evidence.data_team_maturity: 3+ years in Analytics Engineering, Data Engineering, or BI roles; Microsoft Fabric Lakehouse and Warehouse optimization
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Requirements list named modern tools (Spark, Microsoft Fabric, dbt) suggesting mid-level project environments, but absence of a responsibilities section limits confidence; classified as mid.
evidence.jd_authorship: Advanced SQL proficiency including CTEs, window functions, and query optimization; Data quality, validation, monitoring, and observability skills
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: No responsibilities section exists — JD is requirements-only with a generic skills checklist; cannot determine Tuesday morning work; classified as recruiter.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Lakehouse and Warehouse optimization; Power BI semantic model knowledge; Row-Level Security
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Role is technical platform-focused — Lakehouse, semantic models, observability, RLS — serving data consumers rather than a named business function; classified as internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: Portuguese consulting company; 3+ years experience required
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Requirements-only JD with no strategic framing; consulting placement context is execution-oriented by nature; classified as execution.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Lakehouse and Warehouse optimization; CI/CD pipeline experience
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: No responsibilities section to determine dominant verb signal; optimisation language suggests some fix_scale but insufficient information to classify confidently; classified as mixed.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD's requirements-only skills checklist (Spark, Fabric, Power BI, dbt) contains no mention of AI coding tools or GenAI-consuming infrastructure.
evidence.testing_framing: Data quality, validation, monitoring, and observability skills
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality and observability appear as a requirements-list skill rather than a responsibility framed with an explicit ownership verb, matching the tool_listed pattern.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Advanced SQL proficiency including CTEs, window functions, and query optimization
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The JD is a technical skills checklist with no compliance, regulatory, or incident-risk language anywhere, consistent with no loss aversion framing.
